items.label: Getting Started
items.content:
## Welcome!

**ZYNQ is a game + realtime communication library.**

It includes a game database and a WebRTC peer system for chat, calls, and streaming.

items.label: Script Tag
items.content:
### Script Tag
Use this when you want to load ZYNQ directly in the browser via CDN.

```html
<script src="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.0.9/index.min.js"></script>
```

items.label: ES Module
items.content:
### ES Module
Import ZYNQ as a side-effect module (it attaches to window.ZYNQ).

```javascript
import "https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/gh/un-zynq/zynq-lib@1.0.9/index.min.js"
```

items.label: Game Object
items.content:
## Game Object
Represents a single game entry in the ZYNQ library with metadata like name, alias, URL, and device support.

children.label: list
children.content:
### list
Current filtered list of games used for UI rendering.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.list
```

children.label: total
children.content:
### total
Total number of games loaded in the library.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.total
```

children.label: filteredCount
children.content:
### filteredCount
Number of games after applying filters or search.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filteredCount
```

children.label: history
children.content:
### history
List of recently played games stored locally.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.getHistory()
```

children.label:
favorites
Set of user-marked favorite games.
children.content:
### favorites
Stored favorites list (persisted in localStorage).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.favorites
```

children.label: .init()
children.content:
### init()
Loads the game database and prepares filtering and device detection.

```javascript
await ZYNQ.games.init()
```

children.label: .search()
children.content:
### search()
Filters games by name or alias.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.search("alien")
```

children.label: .random()
children.content:
### random()
Returns a randomized subset of games.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.random(5)
```

children.label: .getRandomOne()
children.content:
### getRandomOne()
Returns one random game without modifying the list.

```javascript
const game = ZYNQ.games.getRandomOne()
```

children.label: .shuffle()
children.content:
### shuffle()
Randomizes the current filtered game list order.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.shuffle()
```

children.label: .sortBy()
children.content:
### sortBy()
Sorts games by a given property such as name.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.sortBy("name")
```

children.label: .sortByPopularity()
children.content:
### sortByPopularity()
Sorts games based on play count (if available).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.sortByPopularity()
```

children.label: .getByAlias()
children.content:
### getByAlias()
Finds a game using its unique alias.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.getByAlias("alien-hominid")
```

children.label: .getByCategory()
children.content:
### getByCategory()
Filters games by category name.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.getByCategory("shooter")
```

children.label: .filterSupported()
children.content:
### filterSupported()
Removes games that are not supported on the current device.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filterSupported()
```

children.label: .filterFavorites()
children.content:
### filterFavorites()
Shows only games marked as favorites.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.filterFavorites()
```

children.label: .toggleFavorite()
children.content:
### toggleFavorite()
Adds or removes a game from favorites.

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.toggleFavorite("alien-hominid")
```

children.label: .addToHistory()
children.content:
### addToHistory()
Stores a played game in history (max 50 items).

```javascript
ZYNQ.games.addToHistory("alien-hominid")
```

items.label: Example
items.content:
### Example Usage
Shows how to load, search, filter, and display games.

```javascript
await ZYNQ.games.init()
const results = ZYNQ.games.search("alien").filterSupported().list
console.log(results)
```

items.label: Constructor
items.content:
## Peer System
ZYNQ Peer is a WebRTC wrapper for chat, calls, and streaming with automatic reconnection.

children.label: .connect()
children.content:
### connect()
Opens a data connection to another peer.

```javascript
peer.connect("id")
```

children.label: .send()
children.content:
### send()
Sends data to a peer or broadcast.

```javascript
peer.send("id", "hello")
```

children.label: .call()
children.content:
### call()
Starts a video/audio call.

```javascript
peer.call("id")
```

children.label: .disconnect()
children.content:
### disconnect()
Fully closes both data and media connections and disables auto-reconnect for that peer.

```javascript
peer.disconnect("id")
```

children.label: .close()
children.content:
### close()
Closes all active connections but keeps the peer instance alive.

```javascript
peer.close()
```

children.label: .destroy()
children.content:
### destroy()
Completely destroys the peer instance and clears all state.

```javascript
peer.destroy()
```

children.label: ready
children.content:
### ready
Fired when the peer gets its ID from the signaling server.

```javascript
peer.on("ready", id => {})
```

children.label: open
children.content:
### open
Fired when a data connection is opened with a remote peer.

```javascript
peer.on("open", id => {})
```

children.label: message
children.content:
### message
Triggered when a data message is received.

```javascript
peer.on("message", msg => {})
```

children.label: stream
children.content:
### stream
Triggered when a remote media stream arrives.

```javascript
peer.on("stream", data => {})
```

children.label: close
children.content:
### close
Triggered when a connection is closed.

```javascript
peer.on("close", data => {})
```

children.label: reconnecting
children.content:
### reconnecting
Triggered during automatic reconnection attempts.

```javascript
peer.on("reconnecting", data => {})
```

children.label: error
children.content:
### error
Triggered when any PeerJS or network error occurs.

```javascript
peer.on("error", err => {})
```
